questions.question: What does `defineSlots` add at runtime compared to what it provides at development time?
options: It adds runtime validation that throws if required slots are missing
It adds nothing at runtime: it only provides type checking and autocomplete for slot props
It registers slot names with Vue DevTools for better component inspection
It generates default slot content when the parent does not provide any

questions.question: In a `defineSlots` declaration, what does the function signature `default(props: { item: Item; index: number }): any` mean?
options: The `default` slot accepts a callback that returns an `Item` with its index
The `default` scoped slot receives `{ item: Item, index: number }` as its slot props
The parent must pass exactly one child element with `item` and `index` attributes
The slot's return type is `any` so TypeScript ignores its children's types

questions.question: How do you prevent a slot wrapper element (e.g., `<header>`) from rendering when no content is provided?
options: Use `v-if="$slots.header"` to check if the slot has been provided before rendering the wrapper
Set a default value in `defineSlots` so the slot is always considered provided
Use a `watch` on `$slots` to reactively add or remove the wrapper
